growth_records.strain: SCELSE-1 (enrichment culture, SBR2; ~45% relative abundance, predominant PAO)
growth_records.medium: EBPR sequencing batch reactor (SBR2), acetate-fed synthetic wastewater

up.method: batch amino acid utilization assay (LC-MS/MS + phosphorus release)

sec.method: batch amino acid utilization assay (anaerobic P release)

growth_records.cite: Xiaojing Xie et al. Metagenomic characterization of the metabolism, evolution, and global distribution of Candidatus Accumulibacter members in wastewater treatment plants. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf278. [PMC12766714]
growth_records.snippet: Eleven amino acids were confirmed to support effective phosphorus release, with the highest rates observed for aspartate (21.3 mg P/g MLSS/h) and glutamate (18.5 mg P/g MLSS/h), followed by asparagine (12.9 mg P/g MLSS/h) and glutamine (10.
growth_records.notes: Enrichment co-culture (not a pure isolate); SCELSE-1 is the predominant PAO (~45%). Measured values are anaerobic phosphorus-release RATES per g MLSS (mixed liquor suspended solids), verbatim from Results, used to validate GEM predictions of amino-acid utilization. Eleven amino acids supported P release but only these four highest were quantified in main text. No growth rate, no per-gDW uptake, no medium composition in main text (SI). No arithmetic performed; all values quoted directly.
